info_gaps: progression of swelling over last 5-10 min
any prior intubation for allergic reactions

resource.note: last monitored bed — anaphylaxis case warrants it

time.note: patient has waited 7 min; anaphylaxis needs epi NOW
4 other 'now' tier patients ahead in queue

warnings: Mucosal swelling + wheeze + tachycardia + borderline-low SBP after shellfish in known allergic patient = anaphylaxis criteria met; epinephrine should not be delayed for hives
SpO2 95% and SBP 108 are trending toward decompensation — reassess q5min
Last monitored bed — coordinate with charge nurse

hypotheses.name: Anaphylaxis (shellfish)
supporting_signals: known shellfish allergy with documented exposure (shrimp)

immediate.action: IM epinephrine 0.3 mg anterolateral thigh NOW
immediate.reason: Anaphylaxis criteria met (mucosal + respiratory after known allergen); epi is first-line and time-critical.

constraints_checked.result: none — young adult, no cardiac history

immediate.action: Place on monitor in resus/monitored bed; IV access x2; supine with legs elevated
immediate.reason: Borderline SBP 108 + tachycardia; needs continuous monitoring and IV access for fluids/repeat epi.

immediate.action: Supplemental O2, albuterol neb for wheeze; prepare H1/H2 blockers + IV methylprednisolone as adjuncts
immediate.reason: Active bronchospasm; adjuncts per anaphylaxis protocol after epi.

monitor.action: Reassess vitals + airway q5min for 30 min; watch for biphasic reaction ≥4–6h
monitor.reason: Anaphylaxis can rebound; observation window standard; redose epi q5–15min if symptoms persist.

monitor.action: Repeat dose of IM epinephrine if no improvement in 5–15 min; consider IV epi drip if refractory
monitor.reason: Up to 1/3 of anaphylaxis cases need a second dose.

escalate_or_redirect.action: Notify MD immediately; secure last monitored bed via charge nurse
escalate_or_redirect.reason: Only 1 MD available and last monitored bed — needs coordination given 4 other 'now' patients in queue.

explanation.summary: 23F with known shellfish allergy presents with mucosal swelling (lip/tongue) plus respiratory compromise (wheeze, RR 22) within minutes of shrimp ingestion — meets anaphylaxis criteria. Vitals show tachycardia (HR 110) and borderline SBP (108), suggesting early circulatory involvement. IM epinephrine is indicated NOW; do not wait for hives or hypotension. Tier = now, ESI 1-2.

citations.signal: mucosal swelling + wheeze after shellfish exposure in known-allergic patient
may require life-saving intervention if airway compromises

checklist.task: Administer IM epinephrine 0.3 mg anterolateral thigh
Place on cardiac monitor + pulse ox + IV x2 in monitored bed
MD bedside evaluation; assess airway, decide on second epi dose / IV fluids / steroids
Confirm monitored bed assignment and reshuffle queue if needed
Reassess vitals + airway q5min for first 30 min, then q15min
Observe minimum 4–6h for biphasic reaction before disposition
Discharge teaching: epi-pen technique, allergen avoidance, allergy/immunology referral

why_this_tier: Anaphylaxis is a time-critical diagnosis — minutes matter
Patient meets WAO anaphylaxis criteria: mucosal involvement + respiratory compromise after known allergen
Vitals trending wrong direction (tachycardia, borderline SBP)
Conservative bias: undertreated anaphylaxis = airway loss / arrest
would_downgrade_if: Symptoms fully resolve with single epi dose, vitals normalize, no recurrence over 4-6h obs
Re-evaluation reveals isolated localized angioedema without systemic features
would_upgrade_if: Failure to respond to first epi dose
